title: Bridge Essay: Tailoring Cognitive Behavioral Therapy to Subtypes of Voice-Hearing.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Daimonic voice — voice-hearing continuum, inner speech misattribution, corollary discharge.

**Tailoring Cognitive Behavioral Therapy to Subtypes of Voice-Hearing.**. Front Psychol.. PMID: 26733919. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2015.01933. Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT) for voice-hearing (i.e., auditory verbal hallucinations; AVH) has, at best, small to moderate effects. One possible reason for this limited efficacy is that current CBT approaches tend to conceptualize voice-hearing as ahomogenous experience in terms of the cognitive processes involved in AVH.
